channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This smart baby monitor with dual mode and enhanced alerts from Momcozy will give new parents peace of mind
item.description: The Momcozy 5-inch Dual-Mode Smart Baby Monitor is a great choice for parents who want to keep an eye on their little ones. It's packed with features including motion and cry detection and temperature monitoring, so you can be certain that your baby is in good hands when monitored by Momcozy.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Two AI developer strategies: Hire engineers or let AI do the work
item.description: The stark difference in the way tech giants in China and the US are approaching AI for internal operations was illustrated late this week by separate announcements from Salesforce and Alibaba.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Next-gen nuclear startup plans 30 reactors to fuel Texas data centers
item.description: Last Energy, a nuclear upstart backed by an Elon Musk-linked venture capital fund, says it plans to construct 30 microreactors on a site in Texas to supply electricity to data centers across the state.
item.dc:creator: Alex Pasternack, Fast Company

item.title: TrapC proposal to fix C/C++ memory safety
item.description: TrapC, a fork of the C language, is being developed as a potential solution for memory safety issues that have hindered the C and C++ languages. TrapC is a proposed C-language extension compiler intended to be implemented as a cybersecurity compiler for C and C++ code, said developer Robin Rowe.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Is this the end for electric supercars? More luxury automakers, including Aston Martin, delay plans for EVs
item.description: A big question mark hangs over the future of Aston Martin's debut EV and more electrified product from Maserati.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Firings at US weather and oceans agency risk lives and economy, former agency heads warn
item.description: The people who once ran the federal weather and oceans agency say it touches people’s daily lives in unnoticed ways and that massive firings there could cause needless deaths and a big hit to America’s economy.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rust 1.85 arrives with long-awaited async closures
item.description: Rust 1.85, the latest version of the burgeoning language positioned for speed, safety, and ease of use, has arrived, with new capabilities including async closures. The release also constitutes a stabilization of the Rust 2024 edition of the language.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Hollywood’s obsession with AI-enabled ‘perfection’ is making movies less human
item.description: The notion of authenticity in the movies has moved a step beyond the merely realistic. More and more, expensive and time-consuming fixes to minor issues of screen realism have become the work of statistical data renderings—the visual or aural products of generative artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: A.S. Hamrah, Fast Company

item.title: Tech layoffs this year: A timeline
item.description: 2025 began in turmoil, with layoffs at some of the largest tech companies despite the support shown by the new US administration. 2024 had been a year of recovery, with the pace of layoffs slowing and IT employment the highest for years following two years of massive IT layoff in 2022 and 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: East Bay startup’s new stove that plugs into a normal wall outlet could be major gain for health and climate
item.description: The sleek, standard four-burner electric induction stove runs on 120 volts, meaning there is no need to pay a licensed electrician thousands of dollars to rewire to 240 volts, which many electric stoves require.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Federal Executive Forum Artificial Intelligence Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2025
item.description: How are agencies refining their AI strategy? The post Federal Executive Forum Artificial Intelligence Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: PC sales could be set to fall this year, but Trump tariffs aren't the only reason why
item.description: Trump isn’t the only reason that PC upgrades could be getting costlier and less common, analysts say.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Finally, we have some GPU competition - AMD announces the Radeon RX 9070 XT March 6 launch date, starting at $599 alongside the RX 9070 at $549
item.description: After months of waiting, AMD has finally unveiled the Radeon RX 9070 series GPUs and their pricing, where its XT variant looks set to be the winner of this generation's budget GPUs.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google Enhances AlloyDB to Compete with PostgreSQL
item.description: Google adds new features to its fully managed database-as-a-service (DBaaS) AlloyDB, providing an alternative to PostgreSQL for enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple unveils 'age assurance' technology amid child safety push
item.description: Apple will introduce "age assurance" technology to boost children's privacy and safety on the company's devices, the company announced Thursday as part of its broader push to protect kids online.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: What is Apple Intelligence: everything you need to know about the AI toolkit
item.description: Apple Intelligence is a built-in AI toolkit for iPhone, iPad and Mac users. It’s designed to make life easier through suggestions, automations and personalization. Here’s why it’s so useful.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Singapore arrests three in AI chip fraud case amid US crackdown on exports
item.description: Singapore authorities have charged three men in connection with an alleged fraud scheme involving the illicit procurement of Nvidia’s high-end AI chips. The men, two Singaporeans and one Chinese national, are accused of misrepresenting end-user details to acquire the advanced hardware.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Thousands of misconfigured building access systems have been leaked online
item.description: Crooks could access buildings that should be off limits, or even exploit them to steal sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Federal agencies plan second email asking workers for weekly accomplishments
item.description: Some agencies plan to send a second Saturday email to federal workers asking them for bullet points on what they did this week, a sign at least some departments plan to continue with the controversial practice ignited by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: AI & Data Exchange 2025: GAO’s Lindsey Saul on AI helping speed data analytics, improve oversight
item.description: AI presents opportunities for greater efficiency, but GAO chief data officer says its effectiveness hinges on data quality. The post AI & Data Exchange 2025: GAO’s Lindsey Saul on AI helping speed data analytics, improve oversight first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: We might have our first look at the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 7, but I can’t tell the difference from the Z Flip 6
item.description: Thanks to unofficial CAD renders, we could have our first look at the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 7 – but if the mockups are to be believed, it looks nearly exactly the same as this year’s model.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Meta Fires Employees for Leaking Company Information
item.description: Meta has fired "roughly 20" employees for leaking confidential company information, amid a rise in leaks and controversy over the company's policies and practices.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: Why fast-learning robots are wearing Meta glasses
item.description: AI and robots need data — lots of it. Companies that have millions of users have an advantage in this data collection, because they can use the data of their customers. A well-known example is Google’s reCAPTCHA v2 service, which I recently lambasted.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The rising threat of shadow AI
item.description: Employees in a large financial organization began developing AI tools to automate time-consuming tasks such as weekly report generation. They didn’t think about what could go wrong. Within a few months, unauthorized applications skyrocketed from just a couple to 65.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Precise asset management leads to impactful zero trust initiatives
item.description: Agencies’ attention and efforts are now shifting towards strengthening cybersecurity defenses to address the more challenging aspects of cyber threats. The post Precise asset management leads to impactful zero trust initiatives first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: AI & Data Exchange 2025: Air Force’s Anthony Genatempo, Alexis Bonnell on scaling AI adoption for acquisition
item.description: The Air Force PEO for Cyber and Networks and the Air Force Research Lab collaborated on AI tools to reduce burden and time in the acquisition process.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4
